summary: Frontend-only verification of the new /conversion-review admin dashboard stage. All requested checks pass: dashboard renders with correct data-testid, title and noindex,nofollow meta; all 18 sections + 5 required tables render with the exact expected row counts (journey=8, CTA=29, friction=10, analytics=15, routing=10); 7 audience pathway cards and 8 audience cards render; journey search filter works ("collector" -> 1 row, only matching row visible); /robots.txt is served and contains 'Disallow: /conversion-review' alongside the other admin routes. Smoke regression on the 10 main public pages and the 3 other admin pages all 200 OK with no JS console errors, no horizontal overflow at 390px, canonical URLs preserved, JSON-LD blocks intact, admin pages still noindex,nofollow. POST /api/inquiries with consent:true returned 201 — no regression on the core conversion flow.
critical_code_review_comments: Pre-existing (not a regression in this stage): POST /api/inquiries accepts consent=false and returns 201. If consent must be enforced server-side, add Pydantic validator. Currently consent is only enforced by the frontend form. Not introduced by this stage — flagging for awareness only.
seed_data_creation: Two TEST_ inquiries posted to /api/inquiries (TEST Conv / TEST NoConsent) — safe to leave or clean up.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Conversion-review stage is fully verified. The page lives at /conversion-review, uses Layout (shares nav/footer with /seo-review, /launch-checklist, /migration-review) by design. Row counts to assert against: journey=8, CTA=29, friction=10, analytics=15, routing=10, sections=18, pathways=7, audiences=8. Robots.txt now lists 4 admin routes disallowed. No public-page content was changed in this stage so SEO regression results from iter_19 still hold.
